message:
So my husband has been rolling his eyes at my "smoke alarm check" calendar reminders for literally two years. Test them every six months, I'd say. He'd nod and forget. Last week, we actually tested one together—and it was dead. No batteries, no sound. Nothing.

The smug look on my face was *chef's kiss*. But honestly? I was just relieved we caught it before we needed it.

Turns out a lot of us skip this one because it feels so routine, but that's exactly why it matters. Dead batteries don't announce themselves—they just... exist in the dark, not doing their job.

I grabbed that little refresher guide and we're going through every alarm in the house this month. It took maybe twenty minutes.

Have you tested yours lately? (And if your partner still thinks it's silly, you now have full permission to collect that "I told you so." 😄)
